wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Course Puppy project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as an academic scheduling and resource management dashboard for educational institutions. Document the core functionality including course registration, room booking, event coordination, and natural language search capabilities. Describe the technology stack featuring Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Include the target audience of educational administrators and students. Provide an architectural overview showing the relationship between frontend components, API integrations, and NLP processing. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Layout System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dashboard Layout System components. Document the DashboardLayout component's structure including props interface, child rendering, and active entity management. Explain the Header component's role in entity navigation with activeEntity state and onEntityChange callback handling. Detail the layout hierarchy, container classes, and responsive design patterns. Include component composition examples, prop forwarding, and integration with the overall dashboard architecture. Address styling conventions, accessibility considerations, and customization options for different entity contexts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Base Widget Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the BaseWidget component architecture. Explain the BaseWidget pattern implementation and how it provides a consistent foundation for all widget types. Document the component props interface including title, children, onRefresh, isRefreshing, lastUpdated, and action. Detail the widget header structure with title display, refresh button functionality, and custom action slots. Explain the footer with last updated timestamp display. Document the styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes for consistent visual appearance. Include the refresh animation system and disabled state handling. Provide guidelines for extending BaseWidget and implementing custom widget behaviors while maintaining consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Hierarchy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Course Puppy's component hierarchy architecture. Document the component tree starting from RootLayout down to individual widgets. Explain the DashboardLayout component structure and its role as the main container. Detail the Header component implementation and its integration with the layout system. Document component composition patterns and prop drilling prevention strategies. Include component lifecycle management and rendering optimization techniques. Address component communication patterns between parent and child components. Provide examples of component reusability and modular design principles used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global Filters Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the global filters management system. Document the useGlobalFilters hook implementation including filter state initialization, entity-specific filter storage, and cross-entity filter sharing. Explain the GlobalFilters interface structure with separate filter objects for rooms, events, and courses. Detail filter application methods including applyFilters for programmatic filter setting, clearFilters for resetting filter states, and clearSpecificFilter for targeted filter removal. Document the activeEntity concept and how it determines which filter set is currently active. Include examples of filter persistence across component re-renders and navigation. Explain the relationship between search queries and filter states. Address filter validation, default value handling, and state mutation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Coursedog API Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Coursedog API client implementation. Detail the API client architecture, authentication mechanisms, and request/response handling patterns. Document the FilterParams interface and data transformation logic. Explain error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and timeout configurations. Include examples of API method usage, parameter validation, and response processing. Document the integration patterns with Next.js API routes and how data flows from external sources to the application. Address rate limiting, caching strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Provide troubleshooting guides for common API integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Course Puppy. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, package manager setup (npm, yarn, pnpm, or bun), and basic Next.js knowledge. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation, and environment setup. Document the development server startup process and initial configuration. Include troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Explain the project structure briefly and point users to key directories. Provide verification steps to ensure successful installation and first run. Keep the content accessible to beginners while covering all essential setup information.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search and Filter System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Search and Filter System components. Document the GlobalSearchBar component's natural language processing integration, query parsing workflow, and filter extraction mechanism. Explain the FilterChips component's role in displaying active filters, chip removal functionality, and filter persistence across entity changes. Detail the interaction between search input and filter chips, including state management and user experience patterns. Include examples of supported query patterns, filter types, and integration with the NLP parser. Address accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and responsive behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rooms Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the RoomsWidget component implementation. Explain how the widget integrates with the useRooms hook for data fetching and state management. Document the room data visualization patterns and availability tracking features. Detail the room filtering capabilities and how users can interact with room listings. Explain the integration with the global filter system and how room-specific filters are applied. Document the refresh mechanism and automatic data updates. Include examples of room status indicators, facility features display, and booking coordination features. Provide guidelines for customizing room widget behavior and extending functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Course Puppy's data flow architecture. Document the complete data pathway from user interactions through NLP parsing to API integration and state management. Explain the observer pattern implementation for automatic data refresh and real-time updates. Detail the React Query cache management and server state synchronization. Document the filter propagation mechanism from GlobalSearchBar through useGlobalFilters to API requests. Include data transformation pipelines, error handling strategies, and loading state management. Address performance optimization techniques including caching strategies and background polling mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Data Fetching Hooks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the entity-specific data fetching hooks pattern. Document the useRooms, useEvents, and useCourses hooks implementation showing the consistent approach to data fetching, caching, and state management. Explain the hook signature patterns, parameter handling, and return value structures. Detail the integration with React Query for automatic caching, background refetching, and error handling. Document the filter-to-query transformation process and how global filters are applied to API requests. Include examples of hook usage in components, error state handling, and loading indicators. Explain the relationship between hooks and the underlying API client. Address performance optimizations like query deduplication and cache invalidation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rooms API Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Rooms endpoint (/api/rooms). Document the GET method implementation, query parameter handling, and filter processing logic. Detail all supported query parameters including status, room, building, startDate, endDate, limit, offset, and query. Explain the FilterParams interface usage and data transformation from query strings to API requests. Include request/response examples, error handling patterns, and status code documentation. Document the integration with fetchRooms function and external API communication. Provide usage examples for common filtering scenarios and pagination patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Course Puppy's system design. Describe the high-level architecture featuring Next.js App Router, React components, and server state management with React Query. Document the component hierarchy from DashboardLayout down to individual widgets. Explain the data flow from user interactions through NLP parsing to API integration. Detail the provider pattern implementation for global state management. Include system boundaries showing the separation between frontend presentation and backend API integration. Document the architectural patterns used including component composition, observer pattern for automatic updates, and factory pattern for widget rendering. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, loading states, and real-time data synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Data Display Components suite. Document the DataTable component's implementation including data rendering, sorting mechanisms, pagination controls, and responsive table design. Explain the StatusBadge component's purpose for visual status indicators, color coding, and status mapping logic. Detail the LoadingSpinner component's role in async operation feedback, animation states, and integration patterns. Include component props, styling options, customization hooks, and performance considerations. Provide usage examples for different data types and display scenarios. Address accessibility compliance, screen reader support, and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Events Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EventsWidget component implementation. Explain the event data visualization and scheduling coordination features. Document how the widget integrates with the useEvents hook for real-time event data management. Detail the event filtering system and how users can browse and manage events. Explain the event status tracking and attendee management capabilities. Document the integration with the global search and filter system for event discovery. Include examples of event calendar views, organizer information display, and event scheduling workflows. Provide guidelines for customizing event widget functionality and adding new event-related features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the design patterns implemented in Course Puppy. Document the Provider pattern used for global state management with React Query. Explain the Factory pattern implementation in the widget system for dynamic component rendering. Detail the Observer pattern usage for automatic data refresh and real-time updates. Document the Hook pattern for custom business logic encapsulation. Include the Component Composition pattern and its benefits for modularity. Address pattern selection rationale, implementation details, and maintenance considerations. Provide examples of how these patterns work together to create a scalable and maintainable architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Query Provider
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the React Query provider implementation and configuration. Explain the QueryClient setup, cache configuration, and provider wrapping strategy. Detail the caching policies, stale time configurations, and background refetching settings. Document the provider's role in enabling server state management across the application. Explain cache invalidation strategies, query refetching triggers, and error boundary integration. Include configuration options for optimistic updates, parallel queries, and query persistence. Address performance considerations like cache size limits and garbage collection. Document integration with Next.js App Router and SSR considerations. Provide examples of provider usage in the application layout and component tree structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Events API Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Events endpoint (/api/events). Document the GET method implementation, query parameter processing, and filter construction logic. Detail supported parameters for event filtering and data retrieval. Explain the integration with the Coursedog API client and data transformation patterns. Include comprehensive request/response examples, error handling scenarios, and status code documentation. Document the relationship between global filters and event-specific queries. Provide practical examples of event search patterns and filtering combinations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Course Puppy's core UI components. Document the DashboardLayout component structure and Header implementation. Explain the GlobalSearchBar component's role in natural language processing and filter extraction. Detail the FilterChips component for managing active filters and their persistence. Document the DataTable component for displaying tabular academic data with sorting and pagination. Explain the StatusBadge component for visual status indicators and the LoadingSpinner for async operations. Include component props, events, styling options, and integration patterns. Provide usage examples and customization guidelines. Address responsive design considerations and accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Courses Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the CoursesWidget component implementation. Explain the course registration tracking and schedule management features. Document the integration with the useCourses hook for course data fetching and state synchronization. Detail the course enrollment workflow and how users can manage their course selections. Explain the course filtering system and how students can discover available courses. Document the instructor coordination features and course scheduling integration. Include examples of course status indicators, enrollment limits, and schedule conflict detection. Provide guidelines for extending course widget functionality and integrating with academic scheduling systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Boundaries and Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Course Puppy's system boundaries and external integrations. Document the separation between frontend presentation layer and backend API integration. Explain the API route architecture and server-side processing boundaries. Detail the Coursedog API integration patterns and external service communication. Document the NLP processing boundary and natural language query handling. Include security considerations, authentication patterns, and data validation at system boundaries. Address error handling strategies across system boundaries and fallback mechanisms. Provide guidelines for extending the system with new integrations and maintaining architectural integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Courses API Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Courses endpoint (/api/courses). Document the GET method implementation, query parameter handling, and course-specific filtering logic. Detail all supported parameters for course search and enrollment tracking. Explain the integration with the Coursedog API client and data transformation processes. Include comprehensive examples of course filtering, pagination, and search patterns. Document error handling strategies, response formatting, and status code documentation. Provide usage examples for common course search scenarios and enrollment queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Widget System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Course Puppy's widget system architecture. Explain the BaseWidget component pattern and how it enables consistent widget behavior across different entity types. Document the RoomsWidget implementation for room management and availability tracking. Detail the EventsWidget for event scheduling and coordination. Explain the CoursesWidget for course registration and schedule management. Include the widget lifecycle, data fetching patterns, and integration with custom hooks. Document the factory pattern used for dynamic widget rendering and the common interface shared by all widgets. Provide guidelines for extending the widget system with new entity types and custom widget implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: NLP Parse Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the NLP Parse endpoint (/api/nlp/parse). Document the POST method implementation for natural language query processing. Detail the request body format, query parameter handling, and response structure. Explain the integration with the NLP parser and filter extraction algorithms. Include examples of natural language queries, confidence scoring, and multi-entity detection. Document error handling for malformed queries, parsing failures, and unsupported keywords. Provide usage examples showing how natural language inputs are converted to structured filter parameters. Address performance considerations and optimization strategies for NLP processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Course Puppy's state management system. Document the React Query integration through QueryProvider for server state management and caching. Explain the useGlobalFilters hook for managing cross-entity filters and their persistence. Detail the custom hooks pattern used for data fetching: useRooms, useEvents, and useCourses. Document the automatic data refresh mechanism and polling intervals. Explain state synchronization between components and filter propagation. Include error handling strategies, loading state management, and cache invalidation patterns. Provide examples of state mutations and side effects. Address performance considerations and memory management for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Course Puppy's server-side API endpoints. Document the RESTful API structure with HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for rooms, events, and courses endpoints. Detail the NLP parse endpoint for natural language query processing. Include authentication methods, request headers, and response formats. Document error handling strategies and status codes. Explain the integration with external Coursedog API and data transformation patterns. Include rate limiting considerations and API versioning. Provide client implementation examples and common usage patterns. Address security considerations and data validation. Document the API routing structure and Next.js App Router integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Natural Language Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Course Puppy's Natural Language Processing system. Explain the query parsing algorithm and confidence scoring mechanism. Document the keyword mapping system for status keywords, entity keywords, building patterns, and room specifications. Detail the filter extraction process from natural language queries. Explain the multi-entity detection capabilities and how the system handles complex queries. Include examples of supported query patterns and their corresponding filter outputs. Document the parser's accuracy metrics and confidence thresholds. Provide guidelines for extending keyword mappings and improving parsing accuracy. Address edge cases and error handling in query processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Environment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Course Puppy's configuration system. Document Next.js configuration options including build settings, optimization flags, and environment-specific configurations. Explain TypeScript configuration for strict typing and module resolution. Detail environment variable setup for API endpoints, refresh intervals, and feature toggles. Document Tailwind CSS configuration and custom styling approach. Include production build configuration and asset optimization settings. Explain the QueryProvider configuration for React Query including cache settings and refetch behaviors. Provide environment setup guides for development, staging, and production deployments. Address security considerations for sensitive configuration data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for Course Puppy contributors. Document code quality standards including ESLint configuration and TypeScript best practices. Explain component naming conventions, file organization patterns, and architectural principles. Detail testing strategies for components, hooks, and API integration. Document commit message conventions and branch naming standards. Include code review guidelines and pull request templates. Explain the development workflow from feature creation to deployment. Address performance optimization techniques and accessibility requirements. Provide troubleshooting guides for common development issues. Document the build process and quality assurance checks.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Course Puppy: Academic Scheduling and Resource Management Dashboard

## Project Introduction

**Course Puppy** is a modern academic scheduling and resource management dashboard designed to streamline course registration, room booking, and event coordination for educational institutions. The platform serves as a centralized command center for students, faculty, and administrative staff to efficiently manage academic resources through an intuitive web interface.

### Purpose Statement
The application provides a unified solution for academic resource discovery and management, enabling users to search and filter across courses, rooms, and events using natural language queries while maintaining real-time synchronization with institutional scheduling systems.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Resource Discovery**: Enable intelligent searching across academic resources with natural language processing
- **Real-time Synchronization**: Maintain up-to-date information through automated refresh mechanisms
- **Multi-entity Management**: Support simultaneous management of courses, rooms, and events
- **User Experience**: Deliver responsive, accessible interfaces with comprehensive filtering capabilities

### Target Audience
- Students seeking course registration and room availability information
- Faculty members managing schedules and room bookings
- Administrative staff overseeing campus resource allocation
- Academic coordinators requiring comprehensive resource analytics

## Technical Architecture

The Course Puppy application follows a modern React-based architecture built on Next.js 16, implementing a clean separation of concerns through well-defined layers and modular components.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Coursedog API Client]
    D --> E[External Coursedog API]
    
    F[React Query Provider] --> G[Custom Hooks]
    G --> H[UI Components]
    H --> I[Widgets]
    
    J[NLP Parser] --> K[Filter Extraction]
    K --> L[Query Construction]
    L --> M[API Requests]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application architecture consists of several key layers:

**Presentation Layer**
- Dashboard Layout with responsive design
- Modular widget system for different resource types
- Reusable UI components with consistent styling

**State Management Layer**
- React Query for server state management
- Custom hooks for business logic encapsulation
- Global filter management system

**Data Access Layer**
- API routes for server-side data fetching
- Coursedog API integration with authentication
- Natural Language Processing for query interpretation

**Integration Layer**
- External API communication
- Environment-based configuration management
- Real-time data synchronization

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Provider Pattern**: Centralized state management through React Query provider
- **Hook Pattern**: Custom hooks for reusable business logic
- **Component Composition**: Modular UI components with clear responsibilities
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic widget rendering based on entity type
- **Observer Pattern**: Automatic data refresh with configurable intervals

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Bootstrap**
- [`src/app/layout.tsx`](src/app/layout.tsx) - Root layout with global providers and metadata configuration
- [`src/providers/QueryProvider.tsx`](src/providers/QueryProvider.tsx) - React Query client initialization with caching strategies

**Routing Configuration**
- [`src/app/page.tsx`](src/app/page.tsx) - Main dashboard page with entity switching and widget rendering
- [`next.config.ts`](next.config.ts) - Next.js configuration with minimal customization

### Core Modules

**API Integration Layer**
- [`src/lib/api/coursedog.ts`](src/lib/api/coursedog.ts) - Coursedog API client with authentication and error handling
- [`src/lib/api/types.ts`](src/lib/api/types.ts) - TypeScript interfaces for all data models and API responses

**Business Logic Layer**
- [`src/hooks/useGlobalFilters.ts`](src/hooks/useGlobalFilters.ts) - Global filter state management
- [`src/hooks/useCourses.ts`](src/hooks/useCourses.ts) - Course data fetching with React Query
- [`src/hooks/useEvents.ts`](src/hooks/useEvents.ts) - Event data fetching with React Query
- [`src/hooks/useRooms.ts`](src/hooks/useRooms.ts) - Room data fetching with React Query

**NLP Processing**
- [`src/app/api/nlp/parse/route.ts`](src/app/api/nlp/parse/route.ts) - NLP query parsing API endpoint
- [`src/lib/nlp/parser.ts`](src/lib/nlp/parser.ts) - Natural language query processing engine

### Configuration Approach

**TypeScript Configuration**
- [`tsconfig.json`](tsconfig.json) - Strict TypeScript compilation with path mapping support

**Build Configuration**
- [`package.json`](package.json) - Dependencies including Next.js 16, React 19, and TanStack React Query
- [`eslint.config.mjs`](eslint.config.mjs) - ESLint configuration for code quality
- [`postcss.config.mjs`](postcss.config.mjs) - PostCSS configuration for Tailwind CSS

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**
- `next@16.2.4` - React framework with App Router
- `react@19.2.4` & `react-dom@19.2.4` - Frontend framework
- `@tanstack/react-query@^5.99.0` - Server state management and caching

**Development Dependencies**
- `typescript@^5` - Type safety and development experience
- `tailwindcss@^4` - Utility-first CSS framework
- `eslint@^9` - Code quality and consistency

### Integration Points

**External API Integration**
- [`src/lib/api/coursedog.ts`](src/lib/api/coursedog.ts) - Coursedog API integration with bearer token authentication
- Environment variables: `COURSEDOG_API_KEY`, `COURSEDOG_INSTITUTION_ID`

**Real-time Updates**
- [`src/providers/QueryProvider.tsx`](src/providers/QueryProvider.tsx) - Configurable refresh intervals via `NEXT_PUBLIC_REFRESH_INTERVAL`

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[DashboardLayout] --> B[Header]
    A --> C[GlobalSearchBar]
    A --> D[FilterChips]
    A --> E[RoomsWidget]
    A --> F[EventsWidget]
    A --> G[CoursesWidget]
    
    H[useGlobalFilters] --> I[FilterChips]
    H --> J[GlobalSearchBar]
    
    K[useCourses] --> L[DataTable]
    M[useEvents] --> L
    N[useRooms] --> L
    
    O[QueryProvider] --> P[React Query Cache]
    P --> Q[API Routes]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-entity Resource Management**
The application provides comprehensive management capabilities across three primary resource types: courses, rooms, and events. Each entity type has dedicated widgets with entity-specific filtering and display logic.

**Natural Language Processing**
Advanced NLP capabilities enable users to search using conversational language like "Show me available rooms in the Student Union" or "Find courses taught by Dr. Smith this week."

**Real-time Data Synchronization**
Automatic background refresh ensures users always have access to current information without manual intervention, with configurable refresh intervals.

### Implementation Highlights

**Intelligent Search System**
- [`src/app/api/nlp/parse/route.ts`](src/app/api/nlp/parse/route.ts) - NLP parsing API endpoint
- [`src/lib/nlp/parser.ts`](src/lib/nlp/parser.ts) - Advanced query processing with keyword extraction and confidence scoring

**Modular Widget Architecture**
- [`src/components/widgets/BaseWidget.tsx`](src/components/widgets/BaseWidget.tsx) - Common widget functionality and styling
- [`src/components/widgets/CoursesWidget.tsx`](src/components/widgets/CoursesWidget.tsx) - Course-specific data presentation
- [`src/components/widgets/EventsWidget.tsx`](src/components/widgets/EventsWidget.tsx) - Event-specific data presentation
- [`src/components/widgets/RoomsWidget.tsx`](src/components/widgets/RoomsWidget.tsx) - Room-specific data presentation

**Responsive Data Visualization**
- [`src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx`](src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx) - Flexible table component with sorting and pagination
- [`src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx`](src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx) - Visual status indicators with color coding

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Processing: User Input
    Processing --> Parsing: NLP Analysis
    Parsing --> Filtering: Extract Filters
    Filtering --> Fetching: API Request
    Fetching --> Success: Data Received
    Fetching --> Error: API Error
    Success --> Display: Render Results
    Error --> Display: Show Error Message
    Display --> Idle: User Interaction
```

**Data Flow Architecture**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant S as Search Bar
    participant N as NLP Parser
    participant A as API Route
    participant C as Coursedog API
    participant R as React Query
    
    U->>S: Enter natural language query
    S->>N: Parse query with context
    N->>A: Send parsed filters
    A->>C: Forward filtered request
    C-->>A: Return filtered data
    A-->>R: Cache data with TTL
    R-->>U: Display results with auto-refresh
```

**Component Lifecycle**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Component Mount] --> B[Initialize React Query]
    B --> C[Fetch Initial Data]
    C --> D[Render UI]
    D --> E[User Interaction]
    E --> F[Update Filters]
    F --> G[Refetch Data]
    G --> H[Update Display]
    H --> I[Auto Refresh]
    I --> C
```

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [QueryProvider.tsx](src/providers/QueryProvider.tsx)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)
- [DashboardLayout.tsx](src/components/layout/DashboardLayout.tsx)
- [useGlobalFilters.ts](src/hooks/useGlobalFilters.ts)
- [types.ts](src/lib/api/types.ts)
- [route.ts](src/app/api/courses/route.ts)
- [coursedog.ts](src/lib/api/coursedog.ts)
- [CoursesWidget.tsx](src/components/widgets/CoursesWidget.tsx)
- [useCourses.ts](src/hooks/useCourses.ts)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json)
- [route.ts](src/app/api/nlp/parse/route.ts)
- [parser.ts](src/lib/nlp/parser.ts)

</blog>
